subject: Re: Latency spike alert — ridgeline-queue p99
body: Rachel — adding @tom. Let's correlate the spike with the consumer warmup window. Can you pull the timing of the lag flush from Grafana?\n\n— Amanda

subject: Re: ridgeline-compute worker-pool resize PR #105
body:
Jake — I've gone through the sizing logic. Main concern: what happens when queue depth drops below the minimum threshold while jobs are in-flight? The resize-down path needs a drain guard.

Also the unit tests don't cover the case where maxWorkers == minWorkers. Can you add that?

— Amanda

subject: Re: ridgeline-api token refresh — regression confirmed
body:
Priya — confirmed. The TTL bump in PR #94 changed the token lifetime from 900s to 1800s but the refresh window logic still assumed 900s. Tokens were expiring before the client retried refresh. Fix: update refreshWindowSecs to be 25% of actual TTL.

Want me to open the patch or will you take it?

— Amanda

body:

Senior Backend Engineer

subject: ridgeline-api rate limiting — Redis dependency plan
body:
Priya — following up on the deferred rate limiting work from Q1. The Redis instance is confirmed for Q3. Before we start the implementation, let's align on:

1. Per-client key format: use JWT sub claim or API key ID?
2. Burst allowance: start at 200 req/min steady, 400 burst?
3. 429 body format: RFC 7807 problem detail or custom JSON?

Want to do a 30-min whiteboard this week?

— Amanda

subject: Feedback on Distributed Systems Specialization
body:
Hi Coursera team,

I completed the Distributed Systems Specialization last month. Overall excellent — particularly the Raft consensus module. One piece of feedback: the Go code examples in Week 4 use an older sync/atomic API. Would be great to update them to the slog-structured approach.

Best,
Amanda Hayes

subject: ridgeline-auth service deprecation — Q3 plan
body:
Jake / Priya — let's start discussing the ridgeline-auth deprecation timeline. The auth logic has been migrated to ridgeline-api, but ridgeline-auth is still receiving ~1% of traffic via the legacy route. I'd like to shut it down before EOQ3.

Can you each look at whether any client is still pointing at the legacy endpoint and get back to me by EOW?

— Amanda
